# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template for Azure Key Vault to store application secrets. Set access policies that allow multiple applications and service principals to read and list secrets. Use broad access for development flexibility - we'll tighten policies before production launch.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

resources:
- name: 'keyvault01prd_policyrolebinding'   // Role assignment policy binding context (prod) using the key vault identity principal ID and Key Vault Id, not to be confused with service principals used for application. This role is specific only within a production environment due to tightening down access policies
  type: Microsoft.Authorization/角色定价@2019-06-01   // The management group of the resource provider connection and identity scope are defined in Azure Role Definitions, which contain all services from both providers for consumption usage only by clients who require it using 'Microsoft.Consumption'
  properties:     // Specify your roles here according to requirement or custom policies if required with broad access policy at production time   https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-assignments/role-definitions (Refer doc link for role definition)
    scope: '/subscriptions/{订阅ID}' // Subscription id where the key vault will exist - replace {订阅 ID } with your actual subscription's identifier   https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-assignments/role-definitions (Refer doc link for role definition)
    roleDefinitionName: 'Key Vault SAS Reader Role'  // Key vault identity permission can be defined using these roles here - replace with the appropriate ARM Id of your Azure built in permissions. Refers to https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-assignments/role-definitions
    principalId: '{principal id}'  // This is identity for accessing Key Vault, should be set as per application requirements and service principle's objectid or app registration Id from azuredocumentation (Refer doc link)     https://docs.microsoft.com/(VISIT LINK FOR THE DOCUMENTATION - Replace with appropriate Azure Document Link to access the details of how you can set up identity for Key Vault Access policies and SAS Roles.)
- name: 'keyvault01prd_policyrolebinding2'   // Another role assignment policy binding context (prod) using same key vault principal id, not used as above because tightening down access is mentioned before. This roles defines broad permissions on a more general scale allowing any application to list and read secrets
  type: Microsoft.Authorization/角色定价@2019-06-01   // The management group of the resource provider connection, identity scope are defined in Azure Role Definitions (Refers doc link for role definitions) that contain all services from both providers used only by clients who require it using 'Microsoft.Consumption'
  properties:     /// This part is also about roles and scopes https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-assignments/role-definitions (Refer doc link for role definitions)   // Specify your details here according to requirement or custom policies if required with broad access policy at production time
    scope: '/subscriptions/{订阅ID}'  // Subscription id where the key vault will exist - replace {ORDER ID} placeholder by actual subscription's identifier (Refers doc link for how set up sub-ids)   https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-assignments/role-definitions
    roleDefinitionName: 'Key Vault SAS Reader Role Container Permissions Overrides'  // This is Key vault identity permission can be defined using these roles here - replace with the appropriate ARM Id of your Azure built in permissions. (Refers doc link for how set up reader and list + write access on container)
    principalId: '{principal id}'   /// The user or service principle which should have broad read/list permission, also provide its objectid /app registration ID from azuredocumentation  https://docs.microsoft.com/(VISIT LINK FOR DOCUMENTATION - Replace with appropriate Azure Document Link to access the details of how you can set up identity for Key Vault Access policies and SAS Roles.)
